title: periploca divaricata
intro: Periploca divaricata, also known as Devil's-claw or Wood-vine, is a perennial, woody vine native to the tropical forests of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Periploca divaricata include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
